body:
## Goal Provide a minimal, dependency-free HTTP server in Node.js (ESM) that serves the exact text `Hello, World!` as HTML on `GET /`, listening on the port from the `PORT` environment variable (default `3000`). The implementation is intentionally tiny: a single source file plus `package.json`, using only Node's built-in `node:http` module — no Express, Fastify, or other web framework. ## Spec Use the built-in `node:http` `createServer` API. The request handler routes on `req.method` and `req.url`: - `GET /` → respond `200` with header `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8` and a body containing the exact substring `Hello, World!` (a small valid HTML document is fine, e.g. `

Hello, World!

`). - Any other method/path → respond `404` with a short plain-text body. Resolve the listen port as `Number(process.env.PORT) || 3000` so an unset or empty `PORT` falls back to `3000`. Bind the server and log a single startup line (`Server listening on port `) inside the `listen` callback. `package.json` declares `"type": "module"` (ESM), a `"start"` script that runs `node server.js` (matching the single source file's name), and no runtime dependencies. Keep the file count to exactly the two required artifacts — one source file and `package.json`. ## Changes - `server.js` — creates - `package.json` — creates ## Acceptance - [ ] AC-1: A single ESM source file (server.js) and package.json are the only added artifacts; no external web framework is added as a dependency. - [ ] AC-2: The server listens on the port from process.env.PORT, defaulting to 3000 when PORT is unset or empty. - [ ] AC-3: GET / returns HTTP status 200 with a Content-Type header of text/html and a response body containing the exact text "Hello, World!". - [ ] AC-4: package.json defines a "start" script that launches the server via node. - [ ] AC-5: package.json declares "type": "module" so the source file runs as ESM. ## Verify - PORT=3100 node server.js & sleep 1; curl -si http://localhost:3100/ | grep -i '^HTTP/1.1 200' && curl -si http://localhost:3100/ | grep -i '^content-type: text/html' && curl -s http://localhost:3100/ | grep -q 'Hello, World!'; kill %1 (e2e) - npm run start & sleep 1; curl -s http://localhost:3000/ | grep -q 'Hello, World!'; kill %1 (e2e) - node --check server.js (validate)
title: Minimal Node.js HTTP server serving "Hello, World!" on GET /
